cent: noun. A subunit of currency equal to one-hundredth of the main unit of currency in many countries.
cento: noun. A hotchpotch, a mixture; especially a piece made up of quotations from other authors, or a poem containing individual lines from
client: noun. A customer, a buyer or receiver of goods or services.
cline: noun. A gradation in a character or phenotype within a species, deme, or other systematic group.
clone: noun. A living organism (originally a plant) produced asexually from a single ancestor, to which it is genetically identical.
clot: noun. A thrombus, solidified mass of blood.
coil: noun. Something wound in the form of a helix or spiral.
coin: noun. A piece of currency, usually metallic and in the shape of a disc, but sometimes polygonal, or with a hole in the middle.
col: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Columbia-Wenatchi.
con: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Cofán.
cone: noun. A surface of revolution formed by rotating a segment of a line around another line that intersects the first line.
eel: noun. Any freshwater fish of the order Anguilliformes, which are elongated and resemble snakes.
elect: noun. One chosen or set apart.
election: noun. A process of choosing a leader, members of parliament, councillors, or other representatives by popular vote.
elint: noun. Alternative letter-case form of ELINT.
elite: adjective. Of high birth or social position; aristocratic or patrician.
enol: noun. An organic compound containing a hydroxyl group bonded to a carbon atom which is doubly bonded to another carbon atom.
enolic: adjective. Pertaining to, or derived from, an enol.
entice: verb. To lure; to attract by arousing desire or hope.
entoil: verb. To capture with, or as if with, toils or nets; to ensnare or catch out.
eon: noun. Eternity, the duration of the universe.
ice: symbol. ISO 639-2 /B language code for Icelandic.
icon: noun. An image, symbol, picture, portrait, or other representation, usually as an object of religious devotion.
inlet: noun. A body of water let into a coast, such as a bay, cove, fjord or estuary.
into: preposition. To or towards the inside of.
ion: noun. An atom or group of atoms bearing an electrical charge, such as the sodium and chlorine atoms in a salt solution.
lectin: noun. Any of a class of proteins that bind specific carbohydrates.
lee: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lyélé.
lent: noun. Alternative letter-case form of Lent.
lentic: adjective. Of or pertaining to, or living in still waters (such as lakes, ponds, or wetlands).
leone: noun. A unit of currency of Sierra Leone, divided into 100 cents.
let: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lesing-Gelimi.
lie: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Likila.
lin: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Lingala.
line: noun. A path through two or more points (compare ‘segment’); a continuous mark, including as made by a pen; any path, curved or straight.
lint: noun. Clinging fuzzy fluff that clings to fabric or accumulates in one's pockets or navel etc.
loin: noun. The part of the body (of humans and quadrupeds) at each side of the backbone, between the ribs and hips.
lot: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lotuko.
lotic: adjective. Characterized by flowing water; swiftly flowing; concerned with flowing rivers, streams, etc.
nee: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kumak.
net: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nete.
niece: noun. A daughter of one’s sibling, brother-in-law, or sister-in-law; either the daughter of one's brother (" fraternal niece "), or of
nil: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nila.
nit: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Southeastern Kolami.
not: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nomatsiguenga.
notice: noun. The act of observing; perception.
ole: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Olekha.
oleic: adjective. Of or pertaining to oil, especially to vegetable oil.
olein: noun. Any naturally-occurring greasy or oily substance related to fat.
once: adverb. One and only one time.
one: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 1.
tee: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Huehuetla Tepehua.
teen: noun. Synonym of teenager: a person between 13 and 19 years old (inclusive).
tel: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Telugu.
tele: noun. Alternative spelling of telly (“television, telegraph, telephone, teleport, telecommunication”).
telic: adjective. Tending or directed towards a goal or specific end.
ten: noun. NATO, ICAO, ITU & IMO radiotelephony code for 10, used only with o'clock to indicate direction.
tic: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tira.
til: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tillamook.
tile: noun. A regularly-shaped slab of clay or other material, affixed to cover or decorate a surface, as in a roof -tile, glazed tile, stove
toe: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Tomedes.
toil: noun. Labour, work, especially of a grueling nature.
toile: noun. plain or simple twilled fabric
tole: noun. A decorative metalware having a lacquered or enamelled surface that is painted or gilded.
ton: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Tongan.
tonic: adjective. Pertaining to tension, especially of the muscles.
